<user_query>
You are an experienced full-stack developer who specializes in building production-ready web applications quickly and pragmatically. Your task is to create a comprehensive step-by-step implementation guide for building a personal bookmarks app with the following core features.

**What You're Building**

A web application that combines elements of Linktree and Pocket—a minimal, focused bookmarks manager where:
- Users sign up and log in with email + password
- New sign-ups receive a welcome/confirmation email
- Authenticated users can add, edit, and delete their own bookmarks (each with title, URL, and public/private flag)
- Each user has a unique @handle with a pub
lic profile page showing only their public bookmarks
- A private dashboard displays logged-in users' complete bookmark collection
- Unauthenticated visitors can only view public profiles

**Critical Security Requirement**

Treat data isolation as a core feature, not a UI detail. Assume users will directly probe URLs and APIs. One user must never access, modify, or delete another user's data under any circumstance. Enforce this at the API and database level, not just in the frontend.

**Tech Stack & Constraints**

- **Framework:** Next.js (React)
- **Backend & Database:** Supabase (authentication + database)
- **Email:** Resend (transactional email for sign-up confirmations)
- **Deployment:** Vercel
- **Timeline:** Ship this week—prioritize pragmatic, working solutions over perfection
- **Your Experience Level:** Familiar with web development fundamentals but new to Supabase and Resend; flag gotchas and dependencies specific to these services early so you don't waste debugging time

**Your Deliverable**

Create a step-by-step implementation guide that covers:

1. **Project setup** — Next.js boilerplate, repository structure, environment configuration for Supabase and Resend
2. **Database schema** — tables for users, bookmarks, handles; relationships and constraints; Row Level Security (RLS) policies in Supabase to enforce data isolation at the database level
3. **Authentication flow** — Supabase setup, sign-up with email verification, login, session management in Next.js
4. **Core API endpoints** — all CRUD operations for bookmarks, handle assignment, public profile retrieval; authentication checks enforced at each endpoint
5. **Frontend pages** — signup/login forms, dashboard, bookmark management UI, public profile page; protected routes for authenticated users
6. **Email integration** — Supabase + Resend configuration, welcome email template
7. **Deployment** — Vercel setup, environment variable configuration, testing pre-deploy
8. **Testing checklist** — verification steps to confirm security, functionality, and user flows work end-to-end

Flag any gotchas or common mistakes early—especially those specific to Supabase and Resend integration with Next.js—so developers don't waste time debugging.
</user_query>

input.name: Bookmarks App Guide
input.overview: A greenfield implementation guide for a secure personal bookmarks app in the empty `next-with-ai` repo, using Next.js App Router, Supabase (Auth + Postgres + RLS), Resend (SMTP + welcome email), and Vercel deployment—with security enforced at the database layer first.
input.plan:
# Personal Bookmarks App — Implementation Guide Starting point: [`next-with-ai`](README.md) is an empty repo (README only). Everything below is net-new. --- ## Gotchas to Read First (save hours of debugging) | Area | Gotcha | What to do | |------|--------|------------| | **Supabase SMTP** | Default Supabase email only sends to team members and is rate-limited | Configure **Resend SMTP** in Supabase Dashboard before testing real sign-ups | | **Resend** | Requires a **verified domain** before production sends | Verify domain in Resend first; use `onboarding@resend.dev` only for local smoke tests | | **Resend SMTP** | Host `smtp.resend.com`, port **465**, user `resend`, password = API key | Copy exactly into Supabase → Authentication → Email → SMTP | | **Next.js SSR auth** | Never use `getSession()` on the server for protection | Use `supabase.auth.getUser()` or `getClaims()` in middleware, route handlers, and server components | | **RLS UPDATE** | UPDATE policies require a matching **SELECT** policy | Without SELECT, updates silently affect 0 rows | | **RLS public data** | Two SELECT policies on `bookmarks` (owner + public) are OR'd | Private bookmarks stay hidden from anon users automatically | | **user_metadata** | User-editable; **never** use in RLS or authorization | Store handles in `profiles` table, not JWT metadata | | **service_role key** | Bypasses RLS entirely | Never expose via `NEXT_PUBLIC_*`; avoid in bookmark CRUD routes | | **Auth redirect URLs** | Email confirm links 404 without correct callback route | Add `http://localhost:3000/auth/callback` and production URL in Supabase Auth settings | | **Vercel + cookies** | Cached responses can leak sessions if misconfigured | Do not cache auth routes; Supabase middleware/proxy must run on protected paths | | **Email confirmation** | Users cannot log in until confirmed (if enabled) | Show "check your email" UI after signup; don't treat signup as logged-in | --- ## Architecture Overview ```mermaid flowchart TB subgraph client [Browser] SignupLogin[Signup / Login] Dashboard[Private Dashboard] PublicProfile["Public Profile /u/handle"] end subgraph nextjs [Next.js on Vercel] Middleware[Middleware - session refresh] AuthCallback["/auth/callback"] APIRoutes["/api/bookmarks/*"] ServerComponents[Server Components] end subgraph supabase [Supabase] Auth[Supabase Auth] DB[(Postgres + RLS)] end Resend[Resend SMTP + API] SignupLogin --> Auth Auth -->|"confirm email via SMTP"| Resend AuthCallback --> Auth AuthCallback -->|"optional welcome email"| Resend Middleware --> Auth Dashboard --> APIRoutes Dashboard --> ServerComponents PublicProfile --> ServerComponents APIRoutes --> DB ServerComponents --> DB Auth --> DB ``` **Security model (defense in depth):** 1. **Primary:** Postgres RLS on `profiles` and `bookmarks` 2. **Secondary:** API route handlers verify `auth.getUser()` before mutations 3. **UI:** Middleware redirects unauthenticated users away from `/dashboard` Even if someone crafts curl requests, RLS prevents cross-user access. --- ## Phase 1 — Project Setup ### 1.1 Scaffold Next.js From repo root: ```bash npx create-next-app@latest . --typescript --tailwind --eslint --app --src-dir --import-alias "@/*" ``` Recommended additions: ```bash npm install @supabase/supabase-js @supabase/ssr resend zod npm install -D supabase npx supabase init ``` ### 1.2 Target folder structure ``` src/ ├── app/ │ ├── (auth)/login/page.tsx │ ├── (auth)/signup/page.tsx │ ├── (protected)/dashboard/page.tsx │ ├── (protected)/onboarding/page.tsx # handle setup │ ├── u/[handle]/page.tsx # public profile │ ├── auth/callback/route.ts │ ├── api/bookmarks/route.ts │ ├── api/bookmarks/[id]/route.ts │ ├── api/profiles/[handle]/route.ts │ ├── layout.tsx │ └── page.tsx ├── components/ │ ├── auth/SignupForm.tsx │ ├── auth/LoginForm.tsx │ ├── bookmarks/BookmarkForm.tsx │ ├── bookmarks/BookmarkList.tsx │ └── ui/... ├── lib/ │ ├── supabase/client.ts │ ├── supabase/server.ts │ ├── supabase/middleware.ts │ ├── resend.ts │ └── validators.ts └── middleware.ts supabase/migrations/ .env.local .env.example ``` ### 1.3 Environment variables Create [`.env.example`](.env.example) (commit this; never commit `.env.local`): ```bash # Supabase (Dashboard → Settings → API) NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=https://xxxx.supabase.co NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY=sb_publishable_xxx # or legacy anon key # Server-only — only if needed for admin scripts, NOT bookmark CRUD SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY=sb_secret_xxx # Resend RESEND_API_KEY=re_xxx RESEND_FROM_EMAIL=hello@yourdomain.com # App NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL=http://localhost:3000 ``` ### 1.4 Supabase client utilities Create three helpers per [Supabase Next.js SSR guide](https://supabase.com/docs/guides/auth/server-side/nextjs): - [`src/lib/supabase/client.ts`](src/lib/supabase/client.ts) — `createBrowserClient` for client components - [`src/lib/supabase/server.ts`](src/lib/supabase/server.ts) — `createServerClient` with cookie read/write for Server Components, Actions, Route Handlers - [`src/lib/supabase/middleware.ts`](src/lib/supabase/middleware.ts) — session refresh helper Follow the **current** official guide at implementation time (Supabase recently renamed some middleware/proxy patterns and key types). --- ## Phase 2 — Database Schema & RLS ### 2.1 Create Supabase project 1. [supabase.com/dashboard](https://supabase.com/dashboard) → New project 2. Note project URL and publishable/anon key 3. Enable email auth: Authentication → Providers → Email → confirm email **ON** ### 2.2 Migration: `profiles` + `bookmarks` Create via `supabase migration new init_schema`, then apply in SQL editor or CLI: ```sql -- profiles: one row per auth user, stores @handle create table public.profiles ( id uuid primary key references auth.users (id) on delete cascade, handle text not null, display_name text, created_at timestamptz not null default now(), constraint profiles_handle_format check (handle ~ '^[a-z0-9_]{3,30}$'), constraint profiles_handle_lowercase check (handle = lower(handle)) ); create unique index profiles_handle_key on public.profiles (handle); -- bookmarks create table public.bookmarks ( id uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(), user_id uuid not null references auth.users (id) on delete cascade, title text not null check (char_length(title) between 1 and 200), url text not null check (url ~ '^https?://'), is_public boolean not null default false, created_at timestamptz not null default now(), updated_at timestamptz not null default now() ); create index bookmarks_user_id_idx on public.bookmarks (user_id); create index bookmarks_public_idx on public.bookmarks (user_id) where is_public = true; -- auto-update updated_at create or replace function public.set_updated_at() returns trigger language plpgsql as $$ begin new.updated_at = now(); return new; end; $$; create trigger bookmarks_set_updated_at before update on public.bookmarks for each row execute function public.set_updated_at(); ``` **Design notes:** - Handles stored lowercase with regex validation (no `@` in DB; display as `@handle` in UI) - URL check prevents `javascript:` payloads at DB level - `profiles.id` = `auth.users.id` (1:1, no separate users table needed) ### 2.3 Enable RLS + policies ```sql alter table public.profiles enable row level security; alter table public.bookmarks enable row level security; -- PROFILES create policy "profiles_select_public" on public.profiles for select to anon, authenticated using (true); create policy "profiles_insert_own" on public.profiles for insert to authenticated with check (auth.uid() = id); create policy "profiles_update_own" on public.profiles for update to authenticated using (auth.uid() = id) with check (auth.uid() = id); -- BOOKMARKS create policy "bookmarks_select_own" on public.bookmarks for select to authenticated using (auth.uid() = user_id); create policy "bookmarks_select_public" on public.bookmarks for select to anon, authenticated using (is_public = true); create policy "bookmarks_insert_own" on public.bookmarks for insert to authenticated with check (auth.uid() = user_id); create policy "bookmarks_update_own" on public.bookmarks for update to authenticated using (auth.uid() = user_id) with check (auth.uid() = user_id); create policy "bookmarks_delete_own" on public.bookmarks for delete to authenticated using (auth.uid() = user_id); ``` **Why this is secure:** - User A cannot UPDATE/DELETE User B's bookmarks (`auth.uid() = user_id`) - User A cannot INSERT a bookmark with `user_id = B` (insert check) - Anon users only see rows where `is_public = true` - Authenticated users see their own private bookmarks via `bookmarks_select_own` (owners always see all their bookmarks) ### 2.4 Generate TypeScript types ```bash supabase gen types typescript --local > src/types/database.types.ts ``` --- ## Phase 3 — Authentication Flow ### 3.1 Middleware ([`src/middleware.ts`](src/middleware.ts)) ```typescript // Pseudocode — match current @supabase/ssr guide export async function middleware(request: NextRequest) { const { supabase, response } = createMiddlewareClient(request) await supabase.auth.getUser() // refresh session const isProtected = request.nextUrl.pathname.startsWith('/dashboard') || request.nextUrl.pathname.startsWith('/onboarding') if (isProtected) { const { data: { user } } = await supabase.auth.getUser() if (!user) return NextResponse.redirect(new URL('/login', request.url)) } return response } export const config = { matcher: ['/dashboard/:path*', '/onboarding/:path*', '/auth/:path*'], } ``` Do **not** protect `/u/[handle]` — that's the public profile. ### 3.2 Signup ([`src/app/(auth)/signup/page.tsx`](src/app/(auth)/signup/page.tsx)) Client form calls: ```typescript await supabase.auth.signUp({ email, password, options: { emailRedirectTo: `${process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL}/auth/callback`, }, }) ``` After signup: show **"Check your email to confirm"** — do not redirect to dashboard (user isn't confirmed yet). ### 3.3 Login ([`src/app/(auth)/login/page.tsx`](src/app/(auth)/login/page.tsx)) ```typescript await supabase.auth.signInWithPassword({ email, password }) ``` On success: check if profile exists → redirect to `/dashboard` or `/onboarding`. ### 3.4 Auth callback ([`src/app/auth/callback/route.ts`](src/app/auth/callback/route.ts)) Exchange code for session (PKCE flow): ```typescript const { searchParams, origin } = new URL(request.url) const code = searchParams.get('code') if (code) { const supabase = await createClient() await supabase.auth.exchangeCodeForSession(code) } return NextResponse.redirect(`${origin}/onboarding`) // or dashboard if profile exists ``` ### 3.5 Handle onboarding ([`src/app/(protected)/onboarding/page.tsx`](src/app/(protected)/onboarding/page.tsx)) Post-signup step (after email confirmed): 1. User picks handle (validate client-side + DB unique constraint) 2. Insert into `profiles`: `{ id: user.id, handle }` 3. Redirect to `/dashboard` Use server action or POST `/api/profiles` with authenticated Supabase client (RLS enforces `id = auth.uid()`). ### 3.6 Logout Server action or client call: `supabase.auth.signOut()` then redirect to `/`. --- ## Phase 4 — Core API Endpoints All routes use [`createClient()`](src/lib/supabase/server.ts) (publishable key + user cookies). **RLS is the real enforcement layer.** ### 4.1 Bookmarks CRUD | Method | Route | Auth | Behavior | |--------|-------|------|----------| | GET | `/api/bookmarks` | Required | Returns caller's bookmarks (RLS: own rows) | | POST | `/api/bookmarks` | Required | Create `{ title, url, is_public }`; force `user_id = user.id` server-side | | PATCH | `/api/bookmarks/[id]` | Required | Update own bookmark only | | DELETE | `/api/bookmarks/[id]` | Required | Delete own bookmark only | **Route handler pattern** ([`src/app/api/bookmarks/route.ts`](src/app/api/bookmarks/route.ts)): ```typescript const supabase = await createClient() const { data: { user } } = await supabase.auth.getUser() if (!user) return NextResponse.json({ error: 'Unauthorized' }, { status: 401 }) // Validate body with zod const { data, error } = await supabase .from('bookmarks') .insert({ title, url, is_public, user_id: user.id }) .select() .single() ``` **Security tests to plan for:** - User A PATCHes User B's bookmark UUID → 404 or empty (RLS blocks) - User A POSTs with `user_id: B` → RLS rejects insert check - Unauthenticated GET → 401 from route handler ### 4.2 Public profile API | Method | Route | Auth | Behavior | |--------|-------|------|----------| | GET | `/api/profiles/[handle]` | None | Returns profile + public bookmarks only | Query pattern: ```typescript const { data: profile } = await supabase .from('profiles') .select('handle, display_name, created_at') .eq('handle', handle.toLowerCase()) .single() const { data: bookmarks } = await supabase .from('bookmarks') .select('id, title, url, created_at') .eq('user_id', profile.id) .eq('is_public', true) .order('created_at', { ascending: false }) ``` RLS allows anon SELECT on public bookmarks and all profiles. ### 4.3 Handle update (optional v1.1) PATCH `/api/profiles/me` — allow handle change with uniqueness check. Skip for v1 to ship faster. --- ## Phase 5 — Frontend Pages ### 5.1 Page map | Route | Access | Purpose | |-------|--------|---------| | `/` | Public | Landing + link to login/signup | | `/signup` | Public | Email/password registration | | `/login` | Public | Login form | | `/onboarding` | Auth | Set @handle (first-time) | | `/dashboard` | Auth | Full bookmark CRUD (public + private) | | `/u/[handle]` | Public | Public profile — public bookmarks only | ### 5.2 Dashboard UI ([`src/app/(protected)/dashboard/page.tsx`](src/app/(protected)/dashboard/page.tsx)) Components: - **BookmarkForm** — title, URL, public/private toggle; POST to API - **BookmarkList** — edit inline or modal; PATCH/DELETE via API - Show user's `@handle` with link to `/u/[handle]` - Logout button Use React Query or simple `useEffect` + fetch for v1 (React Query optional). ### 5.3 Public profile ([`src/app/u/[handle]/page.tsx`](src/app/u/[handle]/page.tsx)) Server Component fetching profile + public bookmarks (no auth needed). Display: - `@handle` header - List of public bookmark links (title + external URL) - 404 if handle not found ### 5.4 Styling Tailwind utility classes; minimal Linktree-inspired single-column layout. No design system required for v1. --- ## Phase 6 — Email Integration (Supabase + Resend) Two emails, two mechanisms — keep them separate: ### 6.1 Confirmation email (Supabase Auth → Resend SMTP) **Prerequisite:** Verify domain in Resend. 1. Resend Dashboard → Domains → add DNS records (SPF, DKIM) 2. Supabase → Authentication → Email → **Enable custom SMTP**: - Host: `smtp.resend.com` - Port: `465` - User: `resend` - Pass: `RESEND_API_KEY` - Sender: `RESEND_FROM_EMAIL` 3. Supabase → Authentication → Email Templates → customize **Confirm signup**: - Subject: `Confirm your Bookmarks account` - Body: welcome copy + `{{ .ConfirmationURL }}` link 4. Supabase → Authentication → URL Configuration: - Site URL: `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL` - Redirect URLs: `http://localhost:3000/auth/callback`, `https://your-app.vercel.app/auth/callback` ### 6.2 Welcome email (Resend API — after confirmation) Create [`src/lib/resend.ts`](src/lib/resend.ts): ```typescript import { Resend } from 'resend' export const resend = new Resend(process.env.RESEND_API_KEY) ``` In [`src/app/auth/callback/route.ts`](src/app/auth/callback/route.ts), after successful session exchange: ```typescript await resend.emails.send({ from: process.env.RESEND_FROM_EMAIL!, to: user.email!, subject: 'Welcome to Bookmarks', html: `

Your account is confirmed. Your public page will be at @${handle} once you finish onboarding.

`, }) ``` **Pragmatic v1 shortcut:** If time is tight, skip the separate welcome email and make the Supabase confirmation template serve as both welcome + confirm (single email, ship faster). ### 6.3 Rate limits After enabling custom SMTP, Supabase defaults to ~30 emails/hour. Raise in Supabase → Authentication → Rate Limits before launch if needed. --- ## Phase 7 — Deployment (Vercel) ### 7.1 Pre-deploy checklist ```bash npm run build # must pass locally npm run lint ``` Run security spot-checks locally (see Phase 8). ### 7.2 Vercel setup 1. Push repo to GitHub 2. [vercel.com](https://vercel.com) → Import project 3. Add environment variables (same as `.env.local`, production URLs) 4. Deploy ### 7.3 Post-deploy Supabase config Update Supabase Auth URL Configuration with production domain: - Site URL: `https://your-app.vercel.app` - Redirect URLs: add production `/auth/callback` ### 7.4 Resend production Ensure `RESEND_FROM_EMAIL` uses verified domain (not `onboarding@resend.dev`). --- ## Phase 8 — Testing Checklist ### Security (must pass before launch) - [ ] **Cross-user read:** Log in as User A; try GET `/api/bookmarks` with User B's bookmark ID in PATCH/DELETE — must fail - [ ] **Cross-user write:** POST bookmark with another user's UUID as `user_id` — must fail (RLS) - [ ] **Private leak:** User A has private bookmark; visit `/u/userA` as anon — private bookmark must NOT appear - [ ] **Direct DB probe:** Using Supabase client in browser console with User A session, query User B's private bookmarks — RLS returns empty - [ ] **Unauthenticated dashboard:** Visit `/dashboard` logged out — redirect to `/login` - [ ] **service_role not in client bundle:** Search built JS for service role key — must not appear ### Auth flows - [ ] Signup → confirmation email arrives (Resend dashboard shows delivery) - [ ] Click confirm link → lands on `/auth/callback` → session created - [ ] Login before confirm → appropriate error message - [ ] Login after confirm → dashboard or onboarding - [ ] Logout → session cleared, dashboard inaccessible ### Bookmarks CRUD - [ ] Create bookmark (public + private) - [ ] Edit title, URL, visibility toggle - [ ] Delete bookmark - [ ] Public profile shows only public bookmarks ### Handles - [ ] Duplicate handle rejected (unique constraint) - [ ] Invalid handle format rejected (regex) - [ ] `/u/nonexistent` returns 404 ### Email - [ ] Confirmation email not in spam (check Resend + domain DNS) - [ ] Welcome email sent after confirm (if implemented) --- ## Recommended Build Order (ship this week) | Day | Tasks | |-----|-------| | **Day 1** | Next.js scaffold, Supabase project, env vars, client utilities, middleware | | **Day 2** | Migration (schema + RLS), signup/login/callback, onboarding handle flow | | **Day 3** | Bookmark API routes + dashboard UI | | **Day 4** | Public profile page, Resend SMTP + email templates | | **Day 5** | Security testing, Vercel deploy, production URL config | --- ## Key Files to Create (summary) | File | Role | |------|------| | [`src/lib/supabase/client.ts`](src/lib/supabase/client.ts) | Browser Supabase client | | [`src/lib/supabase/server.ts`](src/lib/supabase/server.ts) | Server Supabase client | | [`src/middleware.ts`](src/middleware.ts) | Session refresh + route protection | | [`supabase/migrations/*_init_schema.sql`](supabase/migrations/) | Tables + RLS | | [`src/app/auth/callback/route.ts`](src/app/auth/callback/route.ts) | Email confirm + optional welcome | | [`src/app/api/bookmarks/route.ts`](src/app/api/bookmarks/route.ts) | Bookmark list/create | | [`src/app/api/bookmarks/[id]/route.ts`](src/app/api/bookmarks/[id]/route.ts) | Bookmark update/delete | | [`src/app/u/[handle]/page.tsx`](src/app/u/[handle]/page.tsx) | Public profile | | [`src/app/(protected)/dashboard/page.tsx`](src/app/(protected)/dashboard/page.tsx) | Private bookmark manager |

content.text:
**Short answer:** Resend without domain verification is not viable for a real multi-user sign-up flow.

**What Resend allows without a domain**

- You can send from `onboarding@resend.dev` using your API key.
- Recipients are limited to **the email on your Resend account**.
- Sending to anyone else returns a **403** — you must verify a domain first.

So Resend’s no-domain mode is a personal sandbox, not a production auth email setup.

**Comparison for your app**

| Provider | Without domain | Who can receive sign-up emails |
|----------|----------------|--------------------------------|
| **Resend sandbox** | `onboarding@resend.dev` | Only your Resend account email |
| **Supabase default** | Built-in SMTP | Org team member emails (add test users in Supabase → Team) |

Supabase default mail is slightly more flexible for testing with a few known addresses.

**Plan updated**

You chose to keep **Supabase built-in mail** for v1. The plan now includes:

- Why Resend sandbox was ruled out
- A **Future: Resend** section for when you verify a domain (SMTP config only — no app code changes)

When you’re ready to open sign-ups to arbitrary users, verify a domain in Resend and plug it into Supabase custom SMTP. Until then, Supabase default mail is the right call.

# Bookmarks

A minimal personal bookmarks app built with Next.js and Supabase.

## Setup

1. Copy environment variables:

```bash
cp .env.example .env.local
```

2. Create a [Supabase project](https://supabase.com/dashboard) and add your URL and publishable key to `.env.local`.

3. Apply the database migration in Supabase SQL Editor (paste contents of `supabase/migrations/20250607000000_init_schema.sql`) or run:

```bash
npx supabase db push
```

4. Configure Supabase Auth (Dashboard):

   - **Authentication → Providers → Email:** Confirm email **ON**, custom SMTP **OFF**
   - **Authentication → Email Templates → Confirm signup:** customize subject/body with welcome copy + `{{ .ConfirmationURL }}`
   - **Authentication → URL Configuration:**
     - Site URL: `http://localhost:3000` (or your production URL)
     - Redirect URLs: `http://localhost:3000/auth/callback`

5. Install and run:

```bash
npm install
npm run dev
```

## Email testing (built-in Supabase mail)

Default Supabase SMTP only sends to **org team member emails**. Sign up with an address on your Supabase org team, or add test addresses under Org → Team.

## Deploy (Vercel)

1. Push to GitHub and import in Vercel
2. Set env vars from `.env.example` (use production `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL`)
3. Add production `/auth/callback` to Supabase redirect URLs

## Scripts

- `npm run dev` — development server
- `npm run build` — production build
- `npm run lint` — ESLint

content.text:
The Personal Bookmarks App is implemented per the plan. Here's what was built:

## What's included

**Stack:** Next.js 16 (App Router), Supabase SSR, Zod, Tailwind CSS 4

**Database** — [`supabase/migrations/20250607000000_init_schema.sql`](supabase/migrations/20250607000000_init_schema.sql)
- `profiles` (handle, display_name) + `bookmarks` (title, url, is_public)
- Full RLS policies for data isolation at the DB layer

**Auth**
- Signup / login / logout
- Email confirmation callback at `/auth/callback`
- Handle onboarding at `/onboarding`
- Middleware session refresh + route protection

**API routes**
- `GET/POST /api/bookmarks` — list/create
- `PATCH/DELETE /api/bookmarks/[id]` — update/delete
- `GET /api/profiles/[handle]` — public profile
- `POST /api/profiles` — create handle (onboarding)

**Pages**
- `/` — landing
- `/dashboard` — private bookmark CRUD
- `/u/[handle]` — public profile (public bookmarks only)

**Verification:** `npm run build` and `npm run lint` both pass.

## Before you run locally

1. Copy env vars: `cp .env.example .env.local`
2. Add your Supabase URL and publishable key
3. Run the migration in Supabase SQL Editor (paste the migration file)
4. Configure Supabase Auth in the Dashboard (see [README.md](README.md)):
   - Confirm email ON, custom SMTP OFF
   - Customize **Confirm signup** template with welcome + `{{ .ConfirmationURL }}`
   - Set redirect URL: `http://localhost:3000/auth/callback`

5. Start dev: `npm run dev`

**Email note:** Supabase built-in mail only sends to org team member addresses — sign up with a team email for testing.

**Deploy:** Push to GitHub → import in Vercel → set env vars → add production callback URL in Supabase.

NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=https://abcdefghijklmnop.supabase.co

NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY=sb_publishable_xxx

Or, if your project still shows the legacy keys:

## Quick path

**Dashboard → your project → Settings → API → Project URL**

Use the exact URL from the dashboard; don’t use the placeholder `https://xxxx.supabase.co` from `.env.example`.

# Personal Bookmarks App — Full Setup Explained

This is how the pieces fit together: **Next.js** (UI + API), **Supabase** (auth + database), and **Postgres RLS** (data isolation).

---

## 1. High-level architecture

```mermaid
flowchart TB
  subgraph browser [Browser]
    Pages[React Pages / Components]
  end

  subgraph nextjs [Next.js on localhost or Vercel]
    MW[Middleware]
    SC[Server Components]
    API[API Route Handlers]
    CC[Client Components]
  end

  subgraph supabase [Supabase Cloud]
    Auth[Auth Service]
    PG[(Postgres + RLS)]
  end

  Pages --> CC
  Pages --> SC
  CC -->|fetch| API
  CC -->|signUp signIn| Auth
  MW --> Auth
  SC --> PG
  API --> PG
  CC --> Auth
  Auth -->|JWT in cookies| MW
```

**Three layers of the app:**

| Layer | Role |
|-------|------|
| **Frontend** | Pages and forms users interact with |
| **Next.js API routes** | Validate input, check auth, talk to Supabase |
| **Supabase Postgres** | Stores data; RLS enforces who can read/write what |

---

## 2. Environment variables — the wiring

From [`.env.example`](.env.example):

```bash
NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=https://your-project.supabase.co   # base URL only
NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY=sb_publishable_xxx       # public client key
NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL=http://localhost:3000                     # for email redirects
```

- **`NEXT_PUBLIC_*`** — exposed to the browser; safe for the publishable key.
- **URL must be the base project URL** — no `/rest/v1/` suffix. The SDK adds paths like `/auth/v1/signup` itself.
- **`SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY`** — optional, server-only, bypasses RLS. Not used in bookmark CRUD.

[`src/lib/supabase/env.ts`](src/lib/supabase/env.ts) reads these and is used by all Supabase clients.

---

## 3. Three Supabase clients — one auth session

Supabase auth uses **cookies**. Three client helpers share the same session:

### Browser client — [`src/lib/supabase/client.ts`](src/lib/supabase/client.ts)
Used in **client components** (`"use client"`):
- Signup / login forms
- Logout button

Runs in the browser and calls Supabase Auth directly.

### Server client — [`src/lib/supabase/server.ts`](src/lib/supabase/server.ts)
Used in **Server Components** and **API routes**:
- Dashboard loading bookmarks
- Public profile page
- `/api/bookmarks`, `/api/profiles`

Reads/writes auth cookies via Next.js `cookies()` so the server knows who is logged in.

### Middleware client — [`src/lib/supabase/middleware.ts`](src/lib/supabase/middleware.ts)
Used in [`src/middleware.ts`](src/middleware.ts) on every matched request:
- Refreshes expired JWTs
- Redirects unauthenticated users away from `/dashboard` and `/onboarding`

**Why three clients?** Same session, different Next.js runtimes (browser vs server vs edge middleware), each with its own cookie API.

---

## 4. Database schema — what gets stored

Migration: [`supabase/migrations/20250607000000_init_schema.sql`](supabase/migrations/20250607000000_init_schema.sql)

### `auth.users` (Supabase built-in)
Created on signup. Holds email, password hash, confirmation status. You don’t manage this table directly.

### `public.profiles` (your app)
One row per user:

| Column | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| `id` | Same UUID as `auth.users.id` |
| `handle` | Unique `@handle` for public URL |
| `display_name` | Optional display name |

Constraints: handle is lowercase, 3–30 chars, `[a-z0-9_]` only.

### `public.bookmarks` (your app)
One row per bookmark:

| Column | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| `user_id` | Owner (FK → `auth.users`) |
| `title`, `url` | Bookmark content |
| `is_public` | If true, visible on `/u/[handle]` |

---

## 5. Row Level Security (RLS) — the security backbone

RLS is enforced **in Postgres**, not just in the UI. Even direct API calls can’t bypass it (without the service role key).

**Profiles:**
- Anyone can **SELECT** (needed for public profiles)
- Only the owner can **INSERT/UPDATE** their own row (`auth.uid() = id`)

**Bookmarks:**
- Owners can **SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE** their own rows
- Anyone can **SELECT** rows where `is_public = true`
- Private bookmarks are hidden from other users and anonymous visitors

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  Request[API Request with user JWT]
  RLS[Postgres RLS Policies]
  Data[(profiles + bookmarks)]

  Request --> RLS
  RLS -->|allowed| Data
  RLS -->|denied| Empty[0 rows / error]
```

**Defense in depth:**
1. **RLS** (primary) — database blocks cross-user access
2. **API routes** — call `getUser()`, return 401 if not logged in
3. **Middleware** — redirect unauthenticated users from protected pages

---

## 6. Complete user journey

### Step 1: Sign up (`/signup`)

[`SignupForm.tsx`](src/components/auth/SignupForm.tsx) calls:

```typescript
supabase.auth.signUp({ email, password, options: { emailRedirectTo: '/auth/callback' } })
```

Flow:
1. Browser → Supabase Auth API
2. Supabase creates row in `auth.users` (unconfirmed)
3. Supabase sends confirmation email (built-in SMTP)
4. UI shows “Check your email” — user is **not** logged in yet

### Step 2: Email confirmation

User clicks link in email → Supabase validates → redirects to:

```
http://localhost:3000/auth/callback?code=...
```

### Step 3: Auth callback (`/auth/callback`)

[`src/app/auth/callback/route.ts`](src/app/auth/callback/route.ts):

1. Exchanges `code` for a session (`exchangeCodeForSession`)
2. Sets auth cookies
3. Checks if `profiles` row exists:
   - **No profile** → `/onboarding`
   - **Has profile** → `/dashboard`

### Step 4: Onboarding (`/onboarding`)

First-time users pick a `@handle`.

[`OnboardingForm.tsx`](src/components/auth/OnboardingForm.tsx) → `POST /api/profiles` → inserts into `profiles`.

RLS ensures `id` must equal `auth.uid()` — users can’t create profiles for others.

### Step 5: Login (`/login`)

[`LoginForm.tsx`](src/components/auth/LoginForm.tsx) calls `signInWithPassword`, then:
- Profile exists → `/dashboard`
- No profile → `/onboarding`

### Step 6: Dashboard (`/dashboard`)

[`dashboard/page.tsx`](src/app/(protected)/dashboard/page.tsx) (Server Component):

1. `getCurrentUser()` — reads session from cookies
2. `getProfileByUserId()` — loads handle
3. Queries all bookmarks for that user (public + private)
4. Renders `DashboardClient` with initial data

**Bookmark CRUD from dashboard:**

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
  participant User
  participant BookmarkForm
  participant API as /api/bookmarks
  participant Supabase
  participant DB as Postgres RLS

  User->>BookmarkForm: Add title, URL, public toggle
  BookmarkForm->>API: POST /api/bookmarks
  API->>API: getUser + Zod validate
  API->>Supabase: insert bookmark user_id=user.id
  Supabase->>DB: RLS checks auth.uid = user_id
  DB-->>API: new row
  API-->>BookmarkForm: bookmark JSON
  BookmarkForm->>User: List updates
```

- **Create:** `BookmarkForm` → `POST /api/bookmarks`
- **Edit:** `BookmarkList` → `PATCH /api/bookmarks/[id]`
- **Delete:** `BookmarkList` → `DELETE /api/bookmarks/[id]`

[`validators.ts`](src/lib/validators.ts) validates title, URL, handle format before DB writes.

### Step 7: Public profile (`/u/[handle]`)

[`u/[handle]/page.tsx`](src/app/u/[handle]/page.tsx) — Server Component, **no login required**:

1. Look up `profiles` by handle
2. Load bookmarks where `is_public = true`
3. Render Linktree-style link list

Private bookmarks never appear here — RLS only returns public rows for unauthenticated/anonymous reads.

### Step 8: Logout

[`LogoutButton.tsx`](src/components/auth/LogoutButton.tsx) calls `supabase.auth.signOut()`, clears cookies, redirects to `/`.

---

## 7. Route map — what each URL does

| Route | Auth | Type | Purpose |
|-------|------|------|---------|
| `/` | Public | Server page | Landing |
| `/signup` | Public | Client form | Register |
| `/login` | Public | Client form | Sign in |
| `/auth/callback` | — | API route | Email confirm → session |
| `/onboarding` | Required | Client form | Set @handle |
| `/dashboard` | Required | Server + Client | Manage all bookmarks |
| `/u/[handle]` | Public | Server page | Public bookmarks only |
| `GET/POST /api/bookmarks` | Required | API | List / create |
| `PATCH/DELETE /api/bookmarks/[id]` | Required | API | Update / delete |
| `POST /api/profiles` | Required | API | Create profile (onboarding) |
| `GET /api/profiles/[handle]` | Public | API | Profile + public bookmarks JSON |

---

## 8. Middleware — what it protects

[`src/middleware.ts`](src/middleware.ts) runs on:

- `/dashboard/*`, `/onboarding/*`
- `/auth/*`
- `/api/bookmarks/*`, `/api/profiles/*`

On each request it:
1. Refreshes the Supabase session from cookies
2. Redirects to `/login` if an unauthenticated user hits `/dashboard` or `/onboarding`

**Not protected:** `/`, `/signup`, `/login`, `/u/[handle]` — public by design.

API routes still enforce auth themselves (401), so middleware is an extra layer for pages, not the only guard.

---

## 9. How data flows for a bookmark operation

Example: User A tries to delete User B’s bookmark.

1. Attacker sends `DELETE /api/bookmarks/{user-b-bookmark-id}` with User A’s session
2. API route: `getUser()` → User A ✓
3. Supabase delete: `.eq("id", id).eq("user_id", user.id)`
4. RLS: row belongs to User B → **no rows deleted**
5. API returns **404** “Bookmark not found”

User A never sees or modifies User B’s data.

---

## 10. External services (Supabase Dashboard config)

These are **not in code** but required for auth email:

| Setting | Location | Purpose |
|---------|----------|---------|
| Confirm email ON | Auth → Providers → Email | Require email verification |
| Email template | Auth → Email Templates | Welcome + `{{ .ConfirmationURL }}` |
| Site URL | Auth → URL Configuration | Base URL for emails |
| Redirect URLs | Auth → URL Configuration | Allow `/auth/callback` |
| SQL migration | SQL Editor | Create `profiles` + `bookmarks` + RLS |

Built-in Supabase email only sends to **org team member addresses** unless you add custom SMTP later.

---

## 11. Mental model — one sentence per piece

- **`.env`** — connects the app to your Supabase project  
- **Supabase clients** — share auth via cookies across browser, server, middleware  
- **`auth.users`** — who can log in  
- **`profiles`** — public identity (`@handle`)  
- **`bookmarks`** — saved links with public/private flag  
- **RLS** — database enforces “you only touch your own data”  
- **Middleware** — keeps strangers off `/dashboard`  
- **API routes** — validate input + check login before DB calls  
- **Client components** — forms and interactive UI  
- **Server components** — load data on the server (dashboard, public profile)  
- **Auth callback** — turns email link into a logged-in session  

---

## 12. Typical first-run checklist

1. Set `.env` with correct Supabase URL (no `/rest/v1/`) and publishable key  
2. Run migration SQL in Supabase SQL Editor  
3. Configure Auth redirect URLs and email template in Supabase Dashboard  
4. `npm run dev`  
5. Sign up with a team-member email → confirm email → onboarding → dashboard → add bookmarks → visit `/u/yourhandle`

If you want to go deeper on one area (auth cookies, RLS policies, or a specific file), say which part and we can walk through it line by line.

import { z } from "zod";

export const handleSchema = z
  .string()
  .trim()
  .toLowerCase()
  .regex(
    /^[a-z0-9_]{3,30}$/,
    "Handle must be 3–30 characters using lowercase letters, numbers, and underscores only.",
  );

export const createProfileSchema = z.object({
  handle: handleSchema,
  display_name: z
    .string()
    .trim()
    .max(100, "Display name must be 100 characters or less.")
    .optional(),
});

export const createBookmarkSchema = z.object({
  title: z
    .string()
    .trim()
    .min(1, "Title is required.")
    .max(200, "Title must be 200 characters or less."),
  url: z
    .string()
    .trim()
    .min(1, "URL is required.")
    .url("Enter a valid URL (e.g. https://example.com).")
    .regex(/^https?:\/\//, "URL must start with http:// or https://."),
  is_public: z.boolean().default(false),
});

export const updateBookmarkSchema = z.object({
  title: z
    .string()
    .trim()
    .min(1, "Title is required.")
    .max(200, "Title must be 200 characters or less.")
    .optional(),
  url: z
    .string()
    .trim()
    .min(1, "URL is required.")
    .url("Enter a valid URL (e.g. https://example.com).")
    .regex(/^https?:\/\//, "URL must start with http:// or https://.")
    .optional(),
  is_public: z.boolean().optional(),
});

export const formatZodError = (error: z.ZodError): string =>
  error.issues[0]?.message ?? "Please check your input and try again.";

content.text:
Updated user-facing messages across the app. Here’s the flow map and what users see now.

## Central messaging

- [`src/lib/messages.ts`](src/lib/messages.ts) — maps Supabase/API errors to plain language
- [`src/components/ui/FormAlert.tsx`](src/components/ui/FormAlert.tsx) — consistent error (red) and success (green) alerts with optional action links

---

## Flow-by-flow messages

### Sign up (`/signup`)
| Situation | Message |
|-----------|---------|
| Success | **Check your email** — confirmation link sent |
| Email already exists | Account exists → **Go to login** link |
| Invalid email/password | Plain-language validation errors |
| Rate limit / network | Friendly retry guidance |

### Log in (`/login`)
| Situation | Message |
|-----------|---------|
| Wrong credentials | “Incorrect email or password…” → **Create an account** link |
| Email not confirmed | “Check your inbox for the confirmation link…” |
| Profile load fails | “We couldn't load your profile…” |

### Onboarding (`/onboarding`)
| Situation | Message |
|-----------|---------|
| Invalid handle | Clear format rules (3–30 chars, etc.) |
| Handle taken | “That handle is taken. Try a different one.” |
| Profile exists | “You already have a profile. Go to your dashboard…” |
| Network error | “Connection problem…” |

### Dashboard bookmarks
| Situation | Message |
|-----------|---------|
| Added | Green **Bookmark added.** |
| Updated | Green **Bookmark updated.** |
| Deleted | Green **Bookmark deleted.** |
| Invalid title/URL | “Title is required.” / “URL must start with http://…” |
| Not found / server error | Friendly fallback messages |
| Empty list | “No bookmarks yet. Add your first one above.” |

### Public profile (`/u/[handle]`)
- 404 page: “This profile does not exist.” (unchanged)

---

## API layer

All API routes now return consistent error keys that map to friendly text on the client — raw Postgres/Supabase errors are no longer exposed to users.

---

Refresh your dev server and walk through signup → login → onboarding → add/edit/delete bookmark to see the new messages. To deploy to Vercel, run `npx vercel deploy --prod`.
